observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (i) regularly reconcile the cashbook with the records of the accounting office to ensure the accuracy and consistency of financial records, and to detect and resolve any discrepancies promptly; and (ii) cause the refund of ₱400.00 charged against the account of the Municipal Government for falling below the required ADB, and regularly monitor the balances of all bank accounts to ensure compliance with the required ADB and avoid the imposition of unnecessary penalties.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year for the establishment of RPTR/SETR based on the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card; and b. Municipal Accountant use the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year from the Municipal Treasurer as basis in the setting up of RPT and SET at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the: a. Inventory Committee responsible for conducting the semi-annual physical count of the Municipal Government inventories prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for all inventory accounts at the end of every semester for submission to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year pursuant to the provision of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; b. General Services Office maintain the required forms and reports for semi-expendable properties pursuant to COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 and COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024; and c. Municipal Accountant submit the corresponding supporting documents for the transactions noted by the Audit Team for audit purposes.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor review the staffing pattern of the Municipal Government to ensure proper segregation of duties, preventing any individual from having simultaneous access to financial transactions and custody of assets, and make appropriate corrective action as necessary; b. General Services Officer (i) prepare and maintain complete property records and reports for PPE and Biological Asset accounts pursuant to Sections 114 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; (ii) ensure that the RPCPPE, RPCLRN and RPCBA are accomplished in accordance with the provision of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and (iii) prepare and maintain Report of Local Road Network and local road inventory pursuant to Sections VI(1) and VII(1) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015; and c. General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer prepare a road map, identify the components of the road and road identification number pursuant to the LRAM Manual as prescribed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-155 dated November 16, 2020; and d. Municipal Engineer (i) provide the Municipal Accountant and General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation and total number of kilometers and the corresponding amount of the Municipality's road networks; and (ii) prepare a schedule of the Local Roads indicating therein the cost of each Road Network Component such as road lot, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures, and other miscellaneous structures to be submitted to the GSO and Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) require all collectors to submit their RAAFs promptly at the end of each month to facilitate the timely preparation and submission to the Audit Team of the CRAAF within five days of the ensuing month, in accordance with Section 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and (b) in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, ensure that the procurement of accountable forms follows the proper procurement procedures as provided under Republic Act No. 9184, as amended, and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that the: (a) demand the concerned accountable officer to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advance amounting to ₱24,692.00 and closely monitor the granting and liquidation of cash advances to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; (b) cause the refund of the excessive payment of traveling expenses of the four Sangguniang Bayan Members totaling ₱960.00; and (c) review and thoroughly examine the claims of Municipal officials and employees to ensure that claims for traveling expenses, either through cash advance or reimbursement, do not result in overpayment in excess of the rates prescribed in EO No. 77.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal PESO (i) submit the quarterly and annual progress reports to the DOLE Provincial or Field Office as required in the MOA, copy furnished the Audit Team; (ii) provide the requested documentation to substantiate the monitoring and supervision activities and proof of DOLE’s approval for the extension; and (iii) ensure compliance with the terms outlined in future agreements, particularly regarding fund liquidation and progress reporting; b. Municipal GSO strictly comply with the provisions of Rule 15 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management and the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs with regard to the proper acceptance of procured goods; c. BAC Chairperson comply with the provision of Section 39.1(c) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the certification from Insurance Commission; and d. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer implement measures to secure and verify updated tax clearances prior to the final settlement of payments for all government contracts procured through public bidding, in line with the requirements of BIR Revenue Regulations No. 017-2024.

We recommended that the: (i) consistently follow up and monitor the processing of documents needed to achieve the approval of the revised 10-year SWMP; (ii) conduct Waste Analysis and Characterization Study for the Barangay Level to determine the composition and volume of wastes generated in each community and participate in Barangay Assemblies for the second semester to disseminate information on the programs of the office, particularly on waste segregation at source to reduce the volume of waste collected, and the promotion, regulation, and limitation of the use of single-use plastics within the municipality; (iii) submit the necessary documents to obtain the ECC for the Sanitary Landfill; (iv) promote ongoing waste segregation at the source through information and education campaigns and regularly assess the funding and resource needs for SWM to ensure the successful implementation of all SWM programs and projects; and (v) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation from barangay officials to support the government's solid waste management program by educating their constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable waste, and utilize material recovery facilities to effectively enforce mandatory segregation at the source.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Mayor consider incorporating the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017 to ensure proper observance of the timeliness of submission and posting of required SEF budget and accountability reports; and ensure that the provisions of the Mayor Executive Order Series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017 be reviewed and updated; and b. MSB Secretariat strictly comply with the requirements on monitoring, transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF, particularly the submission deadlines and reporting format as provided under Item 6.1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017.

We recommended that the: a. MDRRM Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and MDRRM Council provide specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for LDRRMF to evaluate the attainment of the planned PPAs properly, for all the PPAs appropriated in LDRRMF during the AIP; and (ii) ensure that the RSUDRRMF is submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; b. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant (i) transfer to the 20% Development Fund the excess fund of ₱0.214 million that was incorrectly used as source of fund for DRRM Fund projects and strictly comply with Article 417 of the IRR of the LGC, as amended by AO No. 47; and (ii) ensure regular reconciliation of records to present the accurate utilization of the fund during the year; and c. General Services Officer ensure compliance with the provision of Section 118 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs in accepting deliveries of procured goods.

We recommended that the: a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office concerned and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDO) provide specific target date of implementation in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality and provide specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for 20% Development Fund to facilitate easy monitoring and quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; (ii) ensure that PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and (iii) assess and identify issues relevant to timely implementation of PPAs to ensure its completion; and b. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant transfer to the 20% Development Fund the excess fund of ₱0.214 million that was incorrectly used as source of fund for LDRRM Fund projects to be reappropriated to projects consistent with Item Nos. 3.2.1 to 3.2.3 of DBM-DILG-DOF JMC No. 01, dated November 4, 2020 and Section 287 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System: a. Direct the MPDC to maintain a GAD database and other relevant data that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy and formulation and to aid in identifying gender issues focused amongst clients and organizations; b. Prioritize the attendance of the GFPS Members and TWG to various GAD-related trainings and seminars for them to serve as a catalyst in gender mainstreaming in the Municipality and identify gender issues in their GPB; c. Instruct the GFPS to (i) prepare the GAD agenda pursuant to Section C.2 PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 as basis for the annual formulation of PPAs to be included in the Municipality’s GPB; and (ii) ensure submission of GAD AR within the prescribed period under Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, and submit the GAD AR supporting documents pursuant to Section 4.C.8 of the JMC to facilitate the evaluation of the success of the GPB; and d. Instruct the lead offices to prepare the IRR of the GAD Code, as amended, to effectively implement its provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) Create an One-Time Inventory Committee whose main priority is to implement the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances in order to address the issues that are preventing the reconciliation of records between concerned offices and to establish accurate balances of the PPE accounts that are to be presented in the financial statements; (b) Augment the manpower of the General Services Office to enable the effective distribution of tasks and ensure all responsibilities are adequately addressed; and (c) Ensure that the General Services Office maintain property records in accordance with the applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order No. 042 was issued creating the One-Time Cleansing Committee in compliance with AAR 2023, Finding No. 4, page 31. The committee's main goal is to address issues hindering the reconciliation of records between concerned offices and to establish accurate PPE balances. The committee has been given a specific deadline to submit its partial report on or before November 30, 2024. The General Services Office has augmented manpower by hiring a Job Order employee to assist in the effective distribution of tasks and fulfillment of responsibilities. The General Services Office is currently in the process of organizing property records to ensure compliance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.reason: The committee's main goal is to address issues hindering the reconciliation of records between concerned offices and to establish accurate PPE balances. The committee has been given a specific deadline to submit its partial report on or before November 30, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to adopt measures such as availing of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or issue, by judicial action to collect delinquent taxes as provided in RA 7160. Further, agreed to strengthen and intensify its tax collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: In response, the Treasury Office has taken administrative measures including sending demand letters, monthly collections, offering prizes to taxpayers, adopting a One-Stop Shop system, conducting Information, Education, and Communication (IEC) campaigns. However, certain uncontrollable factors contributed to the situation, such as exemption of all 4Ps beneficiaries from paying taxes for three years and the implementation of a policy where only 50% of taxes were collected due to impacts of COVID-19 and Typhoon Odette. Additionally, the e-TRACS was not adjusted to reflect these tax-relief programs, which resulted in a continued high list of collectibles.
observations.reason: The Treasury Office has implemented various administrative measures; however, uncontrollable factors such as tax exemptions and impacts of calamities affected collections.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct all accountable officers:                  (1) to maintain a cashbook shall be reconciled his cashbook balances every end of the month or as the need arises with the subsidiary accounting records; (2) turnover their collections to Liquidating Officer daily and refrain the practice of remitting only when substantial amount is collected or every Friday; (3) deposit collections daily or not later than the next banking day as required by relevant regulations; (4) submit Reconciliation Statement of the unreconciled balances between the General Ledger and Treasury Cashbook duly certified by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer; (5) and we recommend Municipal Treasurer submit immediately to the Office of Auditor the Consolidated RAAF accompanied by the individual RAAFs of concerned accountable officers for CY 2021 and thereafter not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

Further, we recommended that Management agreed to the proposed adjusting entry for the overstatement of Trust Liabilities account, to wit:
Dr. Trust Liabilities ₱3,500.00
Cr. Cash – Local Treasury ₱3,500.00

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher (JEV) on the proposed the adjusting journal entry to record the reconciling items to correct misstatement of accounts. Further, avoid delay in recording possible reconciling items, the Municipal Accountant or Municipal Treasurer secure snapshot in lieu of bank statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, likewise, required the Municipal Assessor to update the list of taxpayers duly certified indicating the amount and collectible for the year 2021 and thereafter and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that Management direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to exert all efforts to trace remaining unreconciled balances. Likewise, locate cannot be found items and if warranted, hold accountable the custodians of the said property. Require the concerned Officers to prepare and maintained Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards and lapsing schedule per asset type wherein every PPE item is depreciated over its useful life. Further, we recommended and Management agreed to stop recording construction materials and labor directly as PPE instead properly classify materials as Inventories when projects are yet to commence and set-up Construction in Progress accounts for every on-going project.                     Further, consult system provider regarding the technical issues encountered. In the meantime, reclassify accounts accordingly, as follows:

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to apply accrual basis of accounting in all financial transactions and refrain from recording payables without actual delivery. For partial deliveries, recognize and record only those items actually received. Likewise, reclassify to appropriate accounts and drop the payables without actual received goods, as follows:

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee to base all procurements with the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) so as not to resort to splitting of requisitions and to conduct competitive bidding if procurement exceeded the threshold amount. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant not to process the transactions if not supported with complete documents.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to submit explanations/justifications on why the total amount of ₱594,329.53 should not be considered irregular. Further require the concerned officer to prepare APP duly transmitted to GPPB and posted in the PhilGEPs. Also, require the Municipal General Services Officer to consolidate the quarterly requirement of each office/department and procure the same from the PS. In case of non-availability thereof, conduct public bidding.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to evaluate the Project Procurement Management Plan as to its consistency with the Municipality’s budget proposal then forward the PPMPs that were included in the budget proposal to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation into an indicative APP. Likewise, the BAC Secretariat forward the PPMP and the consolidated APP to the BAC for final recommendation of the procurement modality. Likewise, the Management direct the Municipal Accountant not to allow disbursements of funds if not supported with complete documentations in pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD 1445, as in this case APP as one of the basic requirements.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the prescribed rules of IRR of RA 10121 and JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.                                         We recommend further that all projects and activities of LDRRMF are based on four (4) thematic areas. Likewise, do not include those items that are administrative expenses in nature, such as traveling expense, television and internet and the likes, because these are under LDRRM Office’s budget. Further, carefully consider events or activities that not all provision of food, meals and snacks are within the scope of disaster preparedness and response.

We recommended that Management require to employ a contract agreement for the Job Order duly authority by the sanggunion concerned upon entering into contracts. Further, we recommend that the LCE and Legislative Office strictly adhere the above provision to prepare ordinance for the augmentation funds from the savings.

We recommended that Management prepare a catch-up plan and fast track the implementation of the above-mentioned priority development projects, refrain from charging the 20% DF of expenditures that do not contribute to the attainment of socio – economic development and environmental management and those that do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator enhance proper planning in the allocation and utilization of 20% DF to ensure proper implementation of the projects and to meet the need of the intended beneficiaries in timely manner.

We recommended that Management substantially utilize the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for the whole year, and prioritize the implementation of laudable projects/programs/activities to attain fully their LDRRM objectives.
We recommended that Management require the MENR Officer to expedite the completion of the building facility storage and utilized the equipment in the accordance with the solid waste management plan.
We recommended that Management enhance effective planning in the implementation of the priority development projects for the benefit of the Municipality of Socorro.

We recommended that Management continue to comply with GSIS regulations of deducting personal share and loan amortization from the salaries of employees and remitting the same within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply in the withholding of the appropriate taxes and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: develop collection strategies to enhance collection of taxes like tax campaign and seek the assistance of barangay officials in the house-to-house campaign; post the notice of delinquency in the payment of RPT with information on the administrative action stated in Sections 254 and 256 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160 in the main entrance of the municipal building and other public places; and prepare the list of all delinquent RPT and submit the same to Sangguniang Bayan with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection of those taxes pursuant to Section 269 of IRR of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) employed the various methods to enforce collection of current and overdue accounts, to wit: Serving of Tax Due Bills and Notice of Delinquencies (NODs) to delinquent taxpayers in coordination with Barangay Officials demanding prompt payment of overdue property taxes. Annual preparation and submission of the List of Delinquent Taxpayers to the PTO to avail administrative action through levy on real property or judicial action as set forth by Section 256 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991. The PTO is vested to execute levy on the defaulted properties reported by the LGUs. Distribution of the List of Delinquent Taxpayers to each of the twenty six (26) barangays of the Municipality. The MTO plans to attend barangay assemblies to raise awareness on the important role of barangays on the collection of real property taxes, benefits from early payment of taxes, and penalties for late settlement of tax dues.
observations.reason: The failure of the Acting Municipal Treasurer to post the Notice of Time for Collection of Tax and the Master List of Delinquent Tax Payers within the vicinity of the Municipal Hall pursuant to Section 249 and Section 254, respectively, of R.A. No. 7160 resulted in low property tax collection efficiency over the years and accumulation of uncollected delinquent property taxes totaling ₱38,307,713.38 as of year-end. Moreover, unavailability of funds for the reconstruction of updated tax maps and total update of property registries hindered the Assessor’s Office in employing efficient tax mapping and assessment.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer informed that the eight units of panel doors were installed at their respective designated area at Two-Storey Municipal Disaster Reduction Management Council (MDRRC) Building.

The Re-Inspection Report for Infrastructure Projects by the COA-TAS dated October 4, 2018 disclosed that the (a) reported 100% accomplishment on the Concreting of Service Road of Socorro Public Memorial Garden based on the submitted SWA was verified with the approved plans and specifications and noted the following deficiencies: (i) no correction was made on the width of the CHB lining from the specified 0.9m to 0.8m. Hence, the previous finding remains amounting to ₱38,452.75; (ii) the CHB lining was re-measured with the aid of walking measure from the beginning up to the project limit and measured back again and found the average length of the structure to be at 545lm, still 5.0 lm short of the 550 lm specified in the approved Plans and Program of Works (POW) with deficiency of ₱7,408.32; (iii) activities done on the restoration of work of back-filing materials was still visible; and (iv) remnants of tree branches, water lilies and over floating debris carried by strong river current and as a result of heavy monsoon rains clogged the dam structure and jamming its gate control mechanism. As such, the COA verified accomplishment for the project is only 99.0% completed in accordance with the approved plans and specifications due to above-mentioned total amount of deficiencies of ₱45,861.07 which represents 0.95% of the total works accomplished.

The COA-TAS re-measured the following: (a) Main Irrigation Canal Phase I based on the color-coded lay-out given by Engr. Matalog as a guide on the extent of the project Phase I. Measurement showed a total length of 698.85 linear meters main irrigation canal more than the length required as per POW of Phase I which is 616.0 linear meters. As to the noted crack at Station 0+542.50 to 0+546.40, the Municipality already repaired the said crack and was now corrected and 100% completed and in accordance with the plans and specifications; and (b) Covered Canal Phase II based on the color-coded lay-out given by Engr. Matalog as a guide on the extent of the project Phase II. Measurement showed a total length of 840.50 linear meters covered canal more than the length required as per POW of Phase II and was now considered 100% completed in accordance with the plans and specifications.
observations.reason: The installation of eight units of panel doors is subject to review and evaluation of the COA-TAS. observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (i) The Municipal Government Officials and member of the Local Development Council properly plan, prioritize all activities and appraise the socio-economic development programs and projects to be implemented during the year, and evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous years; (ii) The Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of those projects in order to ensure their completion within the target date in order to preclude the unwarranted delay of the project completion, anxiety of enjoying the benefits therefrom by the intended beneficiaries; (iii) Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant were provided with a copy of status of projects undertaken by the Municipality; (iv) Municipal Engineer periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, and submit a list of all completed projects to support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of R.A. No. 7160; and (v) The Municipal Engineer provide the status of the projects and ensure their implementation.
observations.managementAction:
The unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects were reverted to Unappropriated Surplus and a supplemental budget was done thru Supplemental Budget No.2-2017, MAO no. 2017. Regular coordination, monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of projects.
The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant were provided with a copy of status of projects undertaken by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE as proof of the conducted physical inventory pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Property Custodian - Designate to maintain and update Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

Not implemented

The Agency still did not conduct inventory for CY 2016.  Reiteration of CY 2014 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance; instruct the Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks; direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the required reconciliation and to perform the same regularly to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements; and adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency and Section 111.2 of P.D. 1445 on the provision of  necessary detail to furnish adequate and correct information.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the financial assistance granted to various schools by debiting receivable accounts instead of having it expensed outright to maintain accountability of the funds; ensure that all financial assistance granted to various schools shall be supported with official receipts.  Instruct all concerned school principals/heads to make a representation before their higher officers/officials to provide the former with official receipts to support receipts; make sure that all financial assistance should be supported with POWs or program/activity design and documents as proofs of implementation to ensure that funds granted to various schools were used for the purpose and as planned/programmed; ensure that charges against the SEF are only for expenses allowed under the law and other regulations.  Submit to the Auditor proofs that payments for salaries and wages for teachers were incurred for the maintenance of extension classes as approved by DepEd Secretary, recommended by DepEd Regional Director and certified by School Division Superintendent of the necessity and urgency to establish extension classes in the municipality.  Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; advise the Municipal Treasurer to name the check under the school or the designation of the school head; and faithfully adhere to Sections 2 and 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 in the grant and utilization of SEF.
observations.reason: Although the Municipal accountant records as a receivable account still various expenses were not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to execute a MOA to cover every cash transfer to implementing agencies and to record the same by debiting a receivable account, instead of an expense account so that therecipients/implementors would still be required to render accounts thereof.  Thus, maintaining accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to advise the municipal officers and employees to incur expenses only if there is corresponding appropriation; stop the charging of prior year’s expenses in the current year.   Expenses incurred in the previous year should be obligated first before the same will be paid in the current year; make sure that all financial transactions are recognized in the books in accordance with the accounting standards to ensure fair presentation of the same in the financial statements; submit justification to the Auditor on the occurrence of the above irregularity.  Failure to do so may cause suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to Sections 4.1 and 119 of P.D. 1445 in the payments, recognition and recording of financial transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents with covering transmittal letter pertaining to the cash advances granted for confidential fund in a manner prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001.  Strictly adhere to the intent of the provisions enshrined in the said Joint Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed that all procurement of the Agency shall be done through competitive bidding as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.    Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow.  Stop the use of Job Order as a mode of procurement.  This was not allowed under the same Act to be used as such.  Strictly adhere to the provision of Section 10 of R.A. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop incurring expenses without corresponding appropriation.  Only expenses with appropriation law or other specific statutory authority shall be paid; utilize the trust fund solely for the purpose in which they are created; and faithfully adhere to the provisions of Sections 4.1 and 4.3 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 1.a.4 and 1.a.5 of CSC Resolution No. 020790 in giving compensations and other benefits to concerned municipal personnel.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of charging against the 20% development fund the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures;  refrain from charging against the fund the expenditures which do not have corresponding appropriation to comply with Section 4.1 of P.D. No. 1445; make corresponding adjustment in the books to correct erroneous entry made in charging a capitalizable transaction to an expense account.  Ensure to comply prevailing accounting rules and standards in recording financial transactions in the books; and strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal use.

We recommended and Management agreed to appropriate at least 5% of the total annual budget for GAD programs, projects and activities as prescribed by the above-mentioned JMC; plan and budget GAD PAPs with due consideration of the amount appropriated thereof; formulate and include only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB and GAR in a manner and in prescribed forms to comply Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; submit to the Auditor justification/explanation why the management incurred expenses that are not in accordance with the objectives of GAD fund, as well as documents to prove that the PPAs indeed addressed the emerging gender issues and concerns.  Failure to comply may result to suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.
Strictly adhere with COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of perfected contracts and purchase orders together with the related supporting documents to COA for review.
